deal_descriptions.short_description: Fluum, an AI-native warm introduction platform, is raising a $2M pre-seed via an open SAFE at a $12M valuation with a 20% discount, following prior capital from angels and family offices.
deal_descriptions.long_description: Fluum is conducting a $2M pre-seed round structured as an open SAFE with a $12M valuation and a 20% discount. Prior to this round the company has raised roughly $400K from angels and family offices. The raise will include the new SAFE commitments alongside that existing investor base; no lead institutional investor is named in the article. Management says the funds will be used to expand the platform and scale its professional network toward targets such as 100,000 decision-makers, 200 B2B/Enterprise users, and $5M ARR. The company has communicated early traction metrics to support the round, which investors can review during the open raise. Fluum is UK-based and led by a founding team with backgrounds in trading, engineering, and growth.

deal_descriptions.short_description: Verustruct, a construction robotics startup, raised $2.4M in an oversubscribed pre-seed round with support from the Yale School of Management and Professor Jennifer McFadden.
deal_descriptions.long_description: Verustruct completed an oversubscribed $2.4 million pre-seed financing round. The raise was supported by the Yale School of Management and individual support from Professor Jennifer McFadden, as noted in coverage of the deal. The financing was described as a pre-seed equity round; no valuation or detailed syndicate members were disclosed in the article. The company has positioned the round to advance its robotic construction technology and commercial strategy. Verustruct is organized as a Public Benefit Corporation, a structure it adopted prior to or during early fundraising. The company is cited as operating out of New Haven and London.

deal_descriptions.short_description: SPORTL, a London-based pay-as-you-train fitness marketplace, raised $320K (£250K) in a pre-seed round.
deal_descriptions.long_description: SPORTL raised $320K (£250K) in a pre-seed funding round to launch its pay-as-you-train marketplace. The article does not list participating investors or additional capital types beyond the pre-seed raise. The funds are intended to build and launch a platform that lets users book workouts at different gym studios across London without long-term memberships. SPORTL’s model is designed to give studios control over pricing and availability while providing them with a distribution channel to fill unused capacity and attract new customers. The company positions itself as a hyper-local, marketplace-driven alternative to membership models and to broader players like ClassPass. No prior funding rounds or operating metrics were disclosed in the coverage.

deal_descriptions.short_description: Yumi Finance, a startup rebuilding credit infrastructure on stablecoin rails, raised a pre-seed round backed by Colosseum and angel investors including Kevin Sekniqi, James Smith, Arnold Lee, Fedor C., Jinsol Bok, Shimon Newman, Valentin Pletnev, Laurens Kubat, Jan Baeriswyl, Sebastien Davies, Julian Rachman, Juhyuk B., Oleg Smagin, Genya Smagin, and Charles Mountain
deal_descriptions.long_description: Yumi Finance announced a pre-seed fundraising to rebuild credit infrastructure on stablecoin rails. The round is composed of early-stage/angel capital from a roster of backers led publicly by Colosseum and a group of individual investors including Kevin Sekniqi, James Smith, Arnold Lee, Fedor C., Jinsol Bok, Shimon Newman, Valentin Pletnev, Laurens Kubat, Jan Baeriswyl, Sebastien Davies, Julian Rachman, Juhyuk B., Oleg Smagin, Genya Smagin, and Charles Mountain. The company did not disclose the total amount raised or a valuation in the announcement. No prior funding rounds were mentioned. Yumi said the funds will support rebuilding credit infrastructure using stablecoins, with initial focus on SMBs and trade finance. The team framed the effort as addressing an estimated $2.5 trillion unmet demand in trade finance and SMB lending in 2025.

deal_descriptions.short_description: Chaseit.ai, a fintech focused on AI-powered automated voice collections, raised an undisclosed pre-seed round.
deal_descriptions.long_description: Chaseit.ai raised a pre-seed round with the amount undisclosed. The round is the company's only reported funding to date; it plans to raise a seed round in the coming months. The startup was founded seven months ago and is headquartered in Vilnius and London. The company processes more than 20,000 automated calls per day (with an expectation of reaching 30,000) and serves financial institutions including Eleving Group. No investors were named in reporting about the pre-seed, and no valuation information was disclosed. The company has stated ambitions to expand beyond collections and support into a full AI-driven credit management platform.
